channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: España confía en sus renovables ante el aumento del precio de los combustibles
item.description: Pedro Sánchez, presidente del gobierno español, dijo que el sistema de energías renovables del país ha amortiguado las consecuencias financieras de la guerra en Irán. Eso no es falso, pero la historia es más compleja.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ovejas pastando en un prado junto a paneles solares, tras un gran apagón en la Península Ibérica, en el pueblo de Hostalric, cerca de Girona, España, el año pasado.

item.title: La manosfera está en declive
item.description: Un nuevo documental muestra que la misoginia en las redes sociales es solo una nueva forma de vender vieja basura.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Grose

item.title: Doctor and Robot
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: How Iranian New Yorkers Are Celebrating Persian New Year During the War
item.description: Nowruz, a joyous holiday marking the new year, has given rise to complicated feelings for some who are fearful for their relatives in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Diba Mohtasham
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: ¿La dieta cetogénica puede mejorar la salud mental?
item.description: El secretario de salud de EE. UU., Robert F. Kennedy Jr., dijo recientemente que la dieta podría “curar” la esquizofrenia. Preguntamos a los expertos sobre esta afirmación.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Christina Caron

item.title: Why I Decided to Share My Cancer Surgery With Times Readers
item.description: An editor on the limits of shyness in the face of serious disease.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Hauser

media:content.media:description: Ethan Hauser’s notebooks, which he used to record medication schedules. They eventually became diaries of sorts.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 20, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Pans Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
item.description: “Let me tell you: There is no doubt in my mind that everything he knows about Pearl Harbor begins and ends with a movie starring Ben Affleck,” Jimmy Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘luminosity’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 15 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: luminosity
item.description: This word has appeared in 15 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Ozempic será medicamento genérico para miles de millones de personas
item.description: En India, China y otros países, Novo Nordisk está a punto de perder la protección de la patente de su exitoso medicamento para adelgazar, lo que podrá permitir versiones competidoras más baratas.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins, Meaghan Tobin, Eshe Nelson, Alex Travelli and Pragati K.B.

media:content.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times

item.title: How Japan Reacted to Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
item.description: Some people criticized President Trump’s decision to invoke a painful chapter of history. Others worried it might harm U.S.-Japan relations.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump met with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Oil Falls and Stocks in Asia Hold Steady
item.description: Oil prices had been gyrating this week, after a new round of attacks on major energy facilities in Iran and Qatar raised concerns about energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Using Charm and Restraint, Japan’s Leader Mostly Avoids Trump’s Wrath
item.description: During her first visit to the White House, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi drew praise from President Trump. But the war in the Middle East will test their relationship.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ms. Takaichi and President Trump in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Spain Says the Sun Shields It From Rising Gas Costs. Is That True?
item.description: Pedro Sánchez, Spain’s prime minister, said the nation’s renewable energy system has softened the financial fallout from the war in Iran. The story is more complex.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Sheep grazing on a pasture next to solar panels, following a major blackout on the Iberian Peninsula, in the village of Hostalric, near Girona, Spain, last year.

item.title: Iran War Fallout: Southeast Asia Hard Hit by Skyrocketing Fuel Prices
item.description: Across Southeast Asia, a region heavily dependent on energy exports brought via the Strait of Hormuz, lives are being upended by higher oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Jason Gutierrez, Muktita Suhartono, Kittiphum Sringammuang and Tung Ngo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fishermen on their boats while docked and unable to go out to sea due to diesel shortages and high prices, in Samut Sakhon, Thailand, on Monday.

item.title: How the Iran War Narrowed Flight Corridors Between Europe and Asia
item.description: One of the few paths left between the two continents threads through Azerbaijan, Georgia and Turkey, as global conflicts complicate aviation logistics.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Planes parked at Dubai International Airport on Wednesday, as airspace closures across the Middle East disrupted flight routes.

item.title: Corrections: March 20, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, March 20, 2026.

item.title: ‘The Alchemist’ Brought Them Together
item.description: But a pandemic breakup became a turning point for Arianna Davis and Jay Santana. He came to appreciate their own love story.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: Scramble for Jet Fuel Shows How Energy Shortages Are Rippling Across Asia
item.description: As China and other major suppliers of refined oil in the region restrict exports, import-dependent countries are desperate to secure fuel supplies.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson, Aaron Krolik, Tung Ngo and Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Wellington International Airport in New Zealand. Air New Zealand has canceled about 1,100 flights through early May.

item.title: ‘I’m Dead. Don’t Send Me Any More Mail.’
item.description: My relationship with my landlord was among the most reliable of my life. I miss her.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Uhler

item.title: He Cooked Up a Surprise Proposal in Mexico
item.description: In 2021, Noah Michelson and Benji Jones began a relatively old-fashioned courtship with FaceTime, walks and waiting.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: A Facebook Friend Request Leads to a Caribbean Connection
item.description: Romario Ricketts, from Jamaica, was moved to reach out to Greshawna Clement, from Guyana, after seeing her at a student demonstration in 2018 and “admired the passion with which she was speaking.”
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: She Calls Him ‘Peter Planner’
item.description: Peter Plante is always planning ahead, including making a secret dinner reservation on his first date with Jill Barry. So, she gave him a nickname.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: Alaska Airlines and FedEx Planes Narrowly Avoid Each Other at Newark Airport
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board said that it was investigating a “close call” that happened as the planes were landing on Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:description: A plane passing the Statue of Liberty on its approach to Newark Liberty International Airport in Newark in January.

item.title: ABC cancela ‘The Bachelorette’ tras video de Taylor Frankie Paul
item.description: La cadena decidió no emitir la nueva temporada luego de que saliera a la luz un video en el que la protagonista ataca a su exnovio.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.category: The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 20, 2026
item.description: Rafael Musa and Rebecca Goldstein open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Brandon Thibodeaux for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Take your seats for today’s titillating themeless puzzle by Rafael Musa and Rebecca Goldstein

item.title: F.C.C. Approves Nexstar’s Acquisition of a Local TV Rival
item.description: The $6.2 billion deal consolidates 265 stations in 44 states and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Daisuke Wakabayashi

media:content.media:description: Tegna was spun off from the Gannett media company in 2015.

item.title: Un petrolero ruso podría ir camino a Cuba, desafiando el bloqueo de EE. UU.
item.description: Si Rusia está intentando enviar petróleo a Cuba, como sospechan algunos analistas, esto representaría un salvavidas fundamental para el gobierno cubano.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Jack Nicas
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: U.S. Says 3 Tied to Silicon Valley Server Maker Broke Export Laws
item.description: Prosecutors said the men, including a co-founder of the company, Super Micro, had diverted servers containing Nvidia A.I. chips to China.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Ana Swanson

media:content.media:description: Super Micro’s exhibit area at the Mobile World Congress in Shanghai in June.

item.title: What to Know About the Sexual Abuse Allegations Against Cesar Chavez
item.description: The accusations of assault have rattled communities across the country that have revered the labor icon for decades.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales, Manny Fernandez, Sarah Hurtes, Laurel Rosenhall and Shawn Hubler

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A mural of Cesar Chavez on a building in Bakersfield, Calif.

item.title: Judge Rules That R.F.K. Jr. Overstepped on Transgender Care
item.description: The ruling provides temporary relief for 21 states seeking to stop the Trump administration from ending federal funding to hospitals that provide gender-transition care.
item.dc:creator: Amy Harmon

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at the White House earlier this year.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Strikes continued in the region as attacks on energy infrastructure rattled global markets.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: An oil refinery on Thursday in Haifa, Israel, that was damaged in an attack.

item.title: On Joe Rogan, Pierre Poilievre Talks Trump, Canada Tariffs and Bruce Lee
item.description: Floundering in the polls, Pierre Poilievre, the leader of Canada’s Conservatives, sat down with the popular podcaster in hopes of raising his standing at home and abroad.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi

media:content.media:description: Pierre Poilievre, leader of Canada’s Conservative party, sat for a two-and-a-half-hour interview with Joe Rogan that was released on Thursday.

item.title: El cuerpo de James Gracey es hallado en Barcelona, según la policí
item.description: La desaparición de James Gracey, de 20 años, estudiante de la Universidad de Alabama, fue reportada el martes. Desapareció durante una visita a un club nocturno en Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe, Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk

item.title: A.I. Is Writing Fiction. Publishers Are Unprepared.
item.description: Book publishing has few safeguards in place to prevent the unwitting publication of a novel heavily generated by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alter

item.title: Nashville Reporter Released From ICE Custody
item.description: Estefany Maria Rodriguez Florez, who works for a Spanish-language outlet, was released on bond in a case that has raised concerns about press freedom.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:description: Estefany Maria Rodriguez Florez, a Nashville reporter detained by immigration agents.

item.title: Iran Combines Real-World Missile Attacks With Online Threats
item.description: Iran launched a missile strike, a disinformation push and a cyberattack targeting Israel all at the same time, analysts say.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Inspecting the aftermath of an Iranian strike in southern Israel on Thursday. Iran also pushed disinformation messaging and launched a cyberattack against Israel on Thursday.

item.title: Is Russian Fuel Headed for Cuba, Testing the U.S. Blockade?
item.description: A Russian oil tanker is being closely tracked to see if it will challenge the Trump administration’s blockade on Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Jack Nicas
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: He Trolls the Left. What Does He Actually Mean?
item.description: Backlash against Jeremy Carl’s incendiary tweets and language caused him to withdraw his nomination for a State Department post. On “Interesting Times,” Ross Douthat challenges him on his use of phrases like “cultural genocide” and “great replacement.”
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: He Thinks Hollywood Has Stigmatized Whiteness
item.description: On “Interesting Times,” Ross Douthat challenges Jeremy Carl on his claim that modern entertainment has stigmatized whiteness.
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: Patriarch Ilia II, a Spiritual Symbol of Stability in Georgia, Dies at 93
item.description: The longest-serving leader in the history of the Georgian Orthodox Church, he helped guide his country in its transition from Soviet repression to modern statehood.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko

media:content.media:description: Thousands accompanied the patriarch’s coffin in a procession on Wednesday to the Holy Trinity Cathedral in Tbilisi.

item.title: Jeff Bezos in Talks to Raise $100 Billion Fund to Transform Companies With A.I.
item.description: The new fund would operate alongside the Amazon founder’s A.I. start-up, Project Prometheus.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Karen Weise

media:content.media:credit: Jeenah Moon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jeff Bezos’ new investment fund could be among the largest in the world.

item.title: Trump’s Handpicked Arts Commission Approves Gold Coin With His Face on It
item.description: Many of America’s founders were fiercely against taking steps that would make its government officials appear like kings, and that included featuring them on the country’s coins.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Charlie Savage

item.title: Cesar Chavez Avenue May Soon Be Gone. Yet to Be Confronted: His Legacy.
item.description: After the revelations of sex abuse, the public is left to make sense of the labor leader’s work and life.
item.dc:creator: Richard Fausset

media:content.media:credit: Jim Thompson/Albuquerque Journal/ZUMA Wire, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Cesar Chavez Day March in 2019 in Albuquerque, N.M.

item.title: White House Seeks to Reassure Americans on Rising Costs
item.description: Also, March Madness begins. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Trump bromea sobre Pearl Harbor en su reunión con la líder de Japón
item.description: Rompiendo un tabú, el presidente Trump importunó en el Despacho Oval a la primera ministra de Japón con una mención al ataque de la Segunda Guerra Mundial.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

item.title: Amid Power Outages, Cubans Worry About Access to Food and Clean Water
item.description: A devastating electricity crisis has made it harder for Cubans to access food, running water and medical care. The island nation mostly relied on Venezuela’s oil to generate its electricity. But in January, the Trump administration ordered Venezuela to stop supplying oil to Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga and Daniel Fetherston

media:content.media:credit: Yamil Lage/Agence France-Presse, via Getty Images

item.title: Horror Novel ‘Shy Girl’ Canceled Over Suspected A.I. Use
item.description: Its publisher, Hachette, will not release the novel in the United States and will discontinue its U.K. edition, citing its commitment to “original creative expression and storytelling.”
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alter

media:content.media:description: The cover of the U.K. edition of “Shy Girl,” by Mia Ballard, which was published by Hachette last year.

item.title: Trump Says He Won’t Send Troops to Iran but Leaves Wiggle Room
item.description: The president was cagey about his plans for Iran. He confirmed the Pentagon was seeking $200 billion to support a protracted war effort while also claiming it would be over soon.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Thursday.
